headlines.title: Russian attacks kill three in Ukraine as Kyiv hits another oil refinery
headlines.snippet: Russian attacks in Ukraine have resulted in three fatalities, while Kyiv has reportedly struck another oil refinery within Russia. President Zelenskyy indicated that Ukraine is expanding its long-range drone operations with new attacks targeting deep inside Russian territory.

headlines.title: Venezuelan sky turns deep red as sun sets over Caracas
headlines.snippet: The sky over Caracas, Venezuela, has taken on a deep red hue during sunset. This natural phenomenon is a visual spectacle occurring as the sun descends.

headlines.title: US-Iran negotiations: What’s the latest?
headlines.snippet: Iranian officials are anticipated to arrive in Doha for discussions with Qatari mediators. This development follows the arrival of US negotiators in the same location.

headlines.title: Rescuers race to reach survivors in Venezuela
headlines.snippet: Rescuers are urgently working to reach individuals trapped in Venezuela. One person expressed a desperate plea for assistance in freeing someone.

headlines.title: Trump official says White House will fight birthright citizenship decision
headlines.snippet: A White House adviser stated that the Trump administration intends to continue challenging decisions regarding birthright citizenship. This stance indicates an ongoing effort to alter the established policy.

headlines.title: Iran-US indirect talks continue in Doha
headlines.snippet: Indirect negotiations between Iran and the United States are continuing in Doha. These discussions, which began Tuesday night, are focusing on the release of Iranian funds and issues related to the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Airlines told to avoid Iraq and Lebanon airspace
headlines.snippet: Airlines have been advised by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) to avoid airspace over Iraq and Lebanon. This warning is due to uncertainty surrounding the US-Iran ceasefire and the potential for rapid escalation.

headlines.title: Iran pushes for formal control over Strait of Hormuz
headlines.snippet: Iran is seeking international recognition for its control over the Strait of Hormuz and its authority to levy charges on vessels. Sources indicate Tehran is prepared to use force if necessary to enforce this claim.

headlines.title: Former Israeli general says Gaza war could have ended earlier
headlines.snippet: A former head of the Israeli army’s Hostages and Missing Persons Command suggests that the war in Gaza could have concluded earlier. He believes some captives might have been returned alive through prompt negotiations.

headlines.title: Iran says foreign ship ran aground in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.snippet: An Iranian state media report indicates a foreign container ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz. The vessel entered shallow waters outside the route designated by Iranian authorities.

headlines.title: Airlines should still avoid Iranian airspace, EU agency says
headlines.snippet: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency is advising airlines to continue avoiding Iranian airspace. This recommendation highlights ongoing safety concerns in the region.

headlines.title: Spain records more than 1,000 heat-related June deaths
headlines.snippet: Spain has reported over 1,000 deaths attributed to heat during the month of June. This indicates a significant impact from the recent heatwave.

headlines.title: Iran was Unable to Export ’Single Barrel of Oil’ During Blockade
headlines.snippet: During a blockade, Iran was reportedly unable to export any oil. This suggests a period of significant disruption to the country's oil exports.

headlines.title: Russia attacks retail fuel stations in Ukraine, killing one, official says
headlines.snippet: Russia has carried out attacks on retail fuel stations in Ukraine, resulting in one fatality. This incident highlights the ongoing conflict and its impact on civilian infrastructure.

headlines.title: Trump announces rare Republican convention ahead of midterms
headlines.snippet: Trump has announced plans for a Republican convention, which is an unusual move ahead of the midterm elections. This event is intended to galvanize support.

headlines.title: Trump weighed 'full-scale' return to war with Iran: report
headlines.snippet: Trump reportedly considered a 'full-scale' return to military action against Iran. However, he has thus far chosen diplomacy as indirect talks resume in Doha.

headlines.title: In occupied southern Lebanon, Netanyahu vows Israel won't leave
headlines.snippet: Netanyahu has stated that Israel will not withdraw from occupied southern Lebanon. He was accompanied by Defense Minister Israel Katz and senior military officials during a recent visit.

headlines.title: Socialist and pro-Palestine Melat Kiros wins Colorado primary
headlines.snippet: Melat Kiros, a 29-year-old former attorney, has won a Colorado primary election. She was previously dismissed after refusing to remove a social media post criticizing law firms' pro-Israel stance.

headlines.snippet: The UN agency for Palestinian refugees is reportedly nearing a 'breaking point.' Such actions jeopardize the well-being of millions of Palestinians and UNRWA staff, with reports of significant fatalities among staffers.

headlines.snippet: Gulf and EU trade talks are gaining momentum as negotiations are revived. The focus is shifting toward sectoral deals amid global economic uncertainty and rising protectionism.

headlines.snippet: Iran and Belarus are seeking to expand their agricultural cooperation. Both nations aim to increase bilateral trade and expedite joint agricultural commission meetings.
